# Example reasoning trace — int_hard_01a_note_derecognition
# Source: v4-dualtrack-8 experiment result
# Generated by: render_full_trace(state)
#
# Transaction: "Ford received a 90-day, non-interest-bearing promissory note with
# a face value of $100,000 as settlement of a trade receivable. Fifty days after
# the note's issue date, Ford discounted the note at the bank at an annual discount
# rate of 15%, receiving the net proceeds in cash after deducting the discount
# charges. Management has determined that this qualifies as derecognition
# (transfer of risks and rewards) under IFRS 9."
#
# Expected: Dr Cash $98,356 / Dr Loss on derecognition $1,644 / Cr Trade receivables $100,000
# Result: MATCH


# Decisions

    ## Ambiguity
        There are 2 potential ambiguities identified in total.
        0 out of 2 of them are unresolved.

        <1> Ambiguity: Sale vs secured borrowing
            - Details: IFRS: Management has explicitly determined this qualifies as derecognition under IFRS 9 — resolve as sale/disposal, not borrowing.
            - Decision: Ambiguity resolved

        <2> Ambiguity: Discount calculation method
            - Details: IFRS: IFRS rules specify 365-day year as default for interest and discount calculations — discount = $100,000 × 15% × 40/365.
            - Decision: Ambiguity resolved

    ## Complexity
        There is 1 potential complexity identified in total.
        0 out of 1 of them is beyond llm capability.

        <1> Complexity: Discount computation for 40 remaining days
            - Details: Best attempt is 
                Dr Cash                                     $   98,356.16
                Dr Loss on Disposal of Note Receivable      $    1,643.84
                Cr Notes Receivable                         $  100,000.00
            - Decision: Complexity within llm capability

    ## Proceed
        Management's stated derecognition determination under IFRS 9 resolves the discounting treatment, and all computation inputs (face value, rate, days remaining) are provided.

    ## Final Decision
        Management's explicit IFRS 9 derecognition determination resolves the sale vs borrowing ambiguity, and the discount computation uses stated inputs with the 365-day default convention.
        Decision: PROCEED


# Classification

    ## Debit Classification
        - asset increase: 1 line associated
            Taxonomy: Cash and cash equivalents
            Reason: Cash received from discounting the note at the bank
        - revenue decrease: 1 line associated
            taxonomy: Gains (losses) on disposals
            Reason: Loss on disposal of financial asset due to discount charges exceeding any premium


    ## Credit Classification
        - asset_decrease: 1 line associated
            taxonomy: trade receivables
            reason: Trade receivable derecognized as risks and rewards transferred per management determination under IFRS 9


# Tax Treatment

No tax mentioned in the transaction. 
Not a taxable supply. 

No tax lines will be added. 

Reasoning: Transaction describes discounting a promissory note at the bank (financial asset derecognition). This is a financial transaction involving the disposal of a receivable, not a taxable supply of goods or services. No tax is mentioned in the text.


<WHY IS THERE TWO TAX NOT MENTIONED in the text????>
<TAX PART is a mess>


# Journal Entry

    - final rationale: Note receivable discounted at bank with derecognition — cash proceeds after discount, loss on disposal recognized, trade receivable removed
    - final entry is as follows:

        Dr Cash and cash equivalents                $   98,356.16
        Dr Loss on disposal of financial asset      $    1,643.84
        Cr Trade receivables                        $  100,000.00
        Total: Dr $100,000.00 / Cr $100,000.00
